name: Non-Buyer
description: Category consumer who hasn't purchased this brand yet
context: Non-Buyer is active in the category but hasn't bought this brand. They may be unaware of it, have misconceptions about it, find it unavailable, perceive it as not for them, or actively prefer competitors. Understanding their barriers is critical—they represent the largest growth opportunity. They're not a monolith: some are gettable with the right message, others are structurally unavailable to you. Focus on increasing mental and physical availability.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Habit-driven and risk-averse. Requires strong disruption to consider switching. Needs familiarity before trial.

alwaysAsks: Why should I switch from what I know?
Is this really better or just different?
Have I even heard of this brand?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates as a skeptical outsider. Needs simple, compelling reasons why this brand exists and why it matters to them.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on barrier reduction, mental availability, clear differentiation, risk mitigation, and compelling switching reasons.

exampleQuotes: I've never heard of that brand
I'm happy with what I'm drinking now
I don't know where to find that
Maybe I'll try it someday, but probably not
keyInsights: Non-buyers are the majority of category consumers for any single brand
Most don't actively dislike you—they just don't think of you
Mental availability matters more than persuasive messaging
Physical availability is often the biggest barrier
Conversion is about distinctive assets and easy availability, not loyalty
Some non-buyers will never buy you and that's okay—focus on the gettable ones
